title: [ipv6_addr.c:269] lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 partial state on error
description:
Contract: lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 should either fully succeed or leave ll_addr_out unchanged.
Violation: On error paths (lines 279, 289, 299), ll_addr_out is zeroed with memset. This modifies output even on failure. Some callers may prefer unmodified output on error (fail-fast pattern) vs zeroed output (fail-safe pattern).
Impact: Low - zeroing is arguably safer (no stale data) but inconsistent with typical contract pattern where output is untouched on error. Currently only called from lichen_l2_iface_init() with NULL ll_addr_out, so dead code path.
Mitigation: Document that ll_addr_out is zeroed on error, or change to not touch output on error.
close_reason: Documented error behavior in ipv6_addr.h: ll_addr_out is zeroed on error to prevent stale data. This is a defensible contract (fail-safe pattern) and the documentation now makes it explicit. The current behavior is consistent across all error paths.
